task_completion.description: Open the draft commercial lease document in LibreOffice Writer

task_completion.description: Apply Heading 1 style to main sections
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the cursor on a main section title (e.g., '1. PARTIES') and the style dropdown in the formatting toolbar displays 'Heading 1'.
Screenshot shows the cursor on a subsection title (e.g., '1.1 Landlord') and the style dropdown in the formatting toolbar displays 'Heading 2'.

task_completion.description: Insert an automatically generated Table of Contents
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a Table of Contents block inserted in the document, typically with a gray background behind the text indicating it is an auto-generated index.

task_completion.description: Insert page numbers in the document footer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the footer area active or visible at the bottom of a page, containing a page number (often with a gray background indicating it is a dynamic field).
The title bar of the LibreOffice Writer window shows 'commercial_lease_final.odt' or a Save As dialog is visible with this exact filename.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Writer GUI to format the document
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with LibreOffice menus, toolbars, and dialogs rather than terminal commands or scripts modifying the .odt file directly.

integrity.description: The Table of Contents is auto-generated, not manually typed
integrity.visual_evidence: The Table of Contents should have the characteristic gray field shading of an auto-generated index in LibreOffice, rather than plain text.

integrity.description: Page numbers are inserted as fields in the footer, not manually typed
integrity.visual_evidence: Page numbers should appear within the designated footer area (often indicated by a blue dashed line when active) and typically have gray field shading.
